overview.summary: This module owns the unified live E2E contract for ephemeral workspace routing, overlay exec, LayerStack lease cleanup, OCC publish, and stale-snapshot conflict behavior. It exercises daemon ops `api.v1.exec_command`, `api.v1.read_file`, `api.v1.write_file`, and `api.v1.command.cancel`. Module config lives at `crates/eos-e2e-test/tests/ephemeral_workspace/config/default.test.yml`.

checklist.title: Per Call Overlay
checklist.description: Every foreground/background exec derives an ephemeral overlay over the latest workspace manifest and only finalized in-workspace deltas become workspace state.

checklist.title: Outside Direct Fs
checklist.description: Writes outside the workspace are excluded from OCC `changed_paths` and remain direct container filesystem effects.

checklist.title: Upperdir Delta
checklist.description: Overlay upperdir accounting stays proportional to modified bytes and does not copy large lowerdir inputs.

checklist.title: Overlay Cleanup
checklist.description: Completed foreground exec releases LayerStack leases, removes overlay scratch, and returns active lease metrics to zero.

checklist.title: Occ Publish Readback
checklist.description: In-workspace exec writes publish through daemon-owned OCC and are readable through `api.v1.read_file`.

checklist.title: Stale Exec Conflict
checklist.description: A long-running exec from a stale snapshot cannot silently overwrite newer direct file content.

checklist.title: Route Edges
checklist.description: Multi-path shell capture, command/publish timing fields, and read-intent no-publish behavior stay observable through daemon responses and audit events.

checklist.title: Policy Denials
checklist.description: Host-prefix writes, cwd escapes, and workspace-destructive shell commands are rejected before any workspace publish when the daemon owns those protocol errors.

checklist.title: Whiteout Resync
checklist.description: File deletes, replacement writes, deep-manifest pre-mount squash, and foreign-published workspace changes round-trip through overlay readback.

checklist.title: Cancel No Partial Publish
checklist.description: Cancelled background execs do not publish partial workspace mutation and still release overlay leases.

checklist.title: Overlay Disk O1
checklist.description: Overlay upperdir accounting stays flat as the lowerdir workspace grows, and overlay run-dir scratch stays bounded and untruncated, proving O(1) overlay disk with respect to workspace size.

test.name: ephemeral_workspace_overlay_publish_and_cleanup
test.description: Groups `exec_simple`, `exec_write_outside_workspace_is_not_captured`, `foreground_exec_recycles_overlay_scratch`, `exec_upperdir_captures_only_the_delta`, and `exec_overlay_mount_publishes_changed_paths`: validates per-call overlay derivation, direct `/tmp` exclusion, delta-sized upperdir accounting, scratch/lease cleanup, OCC publish, and readback.

test.name: ephemeral_workspace_route_edges_and_read_intent
test.description: Uses `exec_multi_path_route_timings_and_read_intent_no_publish`: a multi-path shell write reports every changed path plus command and OCC timings, while a read-only exec over the same paths publishes no changes.

test.name: ephemeral_workspace_whiteout_resync
test.description: Uses `overlay_delete_replacement_write_and_foreign_publish_are_readable`: shell file deletes and replacement writes round-trip through overlay capture, then a later foreign caller publish is readable through the same LayerStack root.

test.name: ephemeral_workspace_cancel_no_partial_publish
test.description: Uses `cancelled_background_exec_does_not_publish_partial_workspace_mutation`: a background command cancelled before its workspace write drains its session and leases without publishing the later file.

test.name: ephemeral_workspace_stale_exec_conflict
test.description: Adds `long_running_exec_conflicts_after_direct_write`: an exec held on an old snapshot cannot silently overwrite newer direct file content, and the newer content remains readable after stale finalization.

test.name: ephemeral_workspace_overlay_disk_o1
test.description: Groups `exec_upperdir_is_flat_across_base_sizes` and `exec_run_dir_scratch_stays_bounded`: a tiny overlay delta over a 15x-growing multi-file lowerdir base keeps `resource.command_exec.upperdir_tree_bytes` flat and delta-sized, while `resource.command_exec.run_dir_tree_bytes` stays bounded and untruncated, proving O(1) overlay disk with respect to workspace size.
